5.strong: he has caused grief/ to her

6.strong: he has caused to be forgotten

8.strong: and/ he has caused to mourn

16.strong: that/ we have waited for/ it

17.strong: and/ he has caused to rejoice

22.strong: he has made an end of/ them

4.strong: he has made to waste away

6.strong: he has caused to dwell/ me

8.strong: and/ I may cry for help

11.strong: and/ he has torn in pieces/ me

12.strong: and/ he has set up/ me

21.strong: and/ you may strip yourself naked

krv.LAM_1_1: 슬프다 이 성이여 본래는 거민이 많더니 이제는 어찌 그리 적막히 앉았는고 본래는 열국 중에 크던 자가 이제는 과부 같고 본래는 열방 중에 공주 되었던 자가 이제는 조공 드리는 자가 되었도다
krv.LAM_1_2: 밤새도록 애곡하니 눈물이 뺨에 흐름이여 사랑하던 자 중에 위로하는 자가 없고 친구도 다 배반하여 원수가 되었도다
krv.LAM_1_3: 유다는 환난과 많은 수고로 인하여 사로잡혀 갔도다 저가 열방에 거하여 평강을 얻지 못함이여 그 모든 핍박하는 자가 저를 쫓아 협착한 곳에 미쳤도다
krv.LAM_1_4: 시온의 도로가 처량함이여 절기에 나아가는 사람이 없음이로다 모든 성문이 황적하며 제사장들이 탄식하며 처녀들이 근심하며 저도 곤고를 받았도다
krv.LAM_1_5: 저의 대적이 머리가 되고 저의 원수가 형통함은 저의 죄가 많으므로 여호와께서 곤고케 하셨음이라 어린 자녀들이 대적에게 사로잡혔도다
krv.LAM_1_6: 처녀 시온의 모든 영광이 떠나감이여 저의 목백은 꼴을 찾지 못한 사슴이 쫓는 자 앞에서 힘없이 달림 같도다
krv.LAM_1_7: 예루살렘이 환난과 군박을 당하는 날에 옛날의 모든 즐거움을 생각함이여 백성이 대적의 손에 빠지나 돕는 자가 없고 대적은 보고 그 황적함을 비웃도다
krv.LAM_1_8: 예루살렘이 크게 범죄하므로 불결한 자 같이 되니 전에 높이던 모든 자가 그 적신을 보고 업신여김이여 저가 탄식하며 물러가도다
krv.LAM_1_9: 저의 더러움이 그 치마에 있으나 결국을 생각지 아니함이여 그러므로 놀랍게 낮아져도 위로할 자가 없도다 여호와여 원수가 스스로 큰 체하오니 나의 환난을 감찰하소서
krv.LAM_1_10: 대적이 손을 펴서 보물을 빼앗았나이다 주께서 이미 이방인을 금하여 주의 공회에 들어오지 못하게 하셨사오나 저희가 성소에 들어간 것을 예루살렘이 보았나이다
krv.LAM_1_11: 그 모든 백성이 생명을 소성시키려고 보물로 식물들을 바꾸었더니 지금도 탄식하며 양식을 구하나이다 나는 비천하오니 여호와여 나를 권고하옵소서
krv.LAM_1_12: 무릇 지나가는 자여 너희에게는 관계가 없는가 내게 임한 근심 같은 근심이 있는가 볼지어다 여호와께서 진노하신 날에 나를 괴롭게 하신 것이로다
krv.LAM_1_13: 위에서부터 나의 골수에 불을 보내어 이기게 하시고 내 발 앞에 그물을 베푸사 나로 물러가게 하셨음이여 종일토록 고적하여 곤비케 하셨도다
krv.LAM_1_14: 내 죄악의 멍에를 그 손으로 묶고 얽어 내 목에 올리사 내 힘을 피곤케 하셨음이여 내가 당할 수 없는 자의 손에 주께서 나를 붙이셨도다
krv.LAM_1_15: 주께서 내 지경 안 모든 용사를 없는 것 같이 여기시고 성회를 모아 내 소년들을 부수심이여 처녀 유다를 술틀에 밟으셨도다
krv.LAM_1_16: 이를 인하여 내가 우니 내 눈에 눈물이 물 같이 흐름이여 나를 위로하여 내 영을 소성시킬 자가 멀리 떠났음이로다 원수들이 이기매 내 자녀들이 외롭도다
krv.LAM_1_17: 시온이 두손을 폈으나 위로할 자가 없도다 여호와께서 야곱의 사면에 있는 자를 명하여 야곱의 대적이 되게 하셨으니 예루살렘은 저희 가운데 불결한 자 같도다
krv.LAM_1_18: 여호와는 의로우시도다 내가 여호와의 명령을 거역하였도다 너희 모든 백성들아 내 말을 듣고 내 근심을 볼지어다 나의 처녀와 소년들이 사로잡혀 갔도다
krv.LAM_1_19: 내가 내 사랑하는 자를 불렀으나 저희가 나를 속였으며 나의 제사장들과 장로들은 소성시킬 식물을 구하다가 성중에서 기절하였도다
krv.LAM_1_20: 여호와여 돌아보옵소서 내가 환난 중에서 마음이 괴롭고 마음이 번뇌하오니 나의 패역이 심히 큼이니이다 밖으로는 칼의 살륙이 있고 집에는 사망 같은 것이 있나이다
krv.LAM_1_21: 저희가 나의 탄식을 들었으나 나를 위로하는 자가 없고 나의 모든 원수가 나의 재앙을 들었으나 주께서 이렇게 행하심을 기뻐하나이다 주께서 반포하신 날을 이르게 하시리니 저희가 나와 같이 되겠나이다
krv.LAM_1_22: 저희 모든 악을 주 앞에 나타내시고 나의 모든 죄악을 인하여 내게 행하신 것 같이 저희에게 행하옵소서 나의 탄식이 많고 나의 마음이 곤비하니이다
krv.LAM_2_1: 슬프다 주께서 어찌 그리 진노하사 처녀 시온을 구름으로 덮으셨는고 이스라엘의 아름다운 것을 하늘에서 땅에 던지셨음이여 진노하신 날에 그 발등상을 기억지 아니하셨도다
krv.LAM_2_2: 주께서 야곱의 모든 거처를 삼키시고 긍휼히 여기지 아니하셨음이여 노하사 처녀 유다의 견고한 성을 헐어 땅에 엎으시고 나라와 방백으로 욕되게 하셨도다
krv.LAM_2_3: 맹렬한 진노로 이스라엘 모든 뿔을 자르셨음이여 원수 앞에서 오른손을 거두시고 맹렬한 불이 사방으로 사름 같이 야곱을 사르셨도다
krv.LAM_2_4: 원수 같이 활을 당기고 대적처럼 오른손을 들고 서서 눈에 아름다운 모든 자를 살륙하셨음이여 처녀 시온의 장막에 노를 불처럼 쏟으셨도다
krv.LAM_2_5: 주께서 원수 같이 되어 이스라엘을 삼키셨음이여 모든 궁을 삼키셨고 견고한 성들을 훼파하사 처녀 유다에 근심과 애통을 더하셨도다
krv.LAM_2_6: 성막을 동산의 초막 같이 헐어 버리시며 공회 처소를 훼파하셨도다 여호와께서 시온 가운데서 절기와 안식일을 잊어버리게 하시며 진노하사 왕과 제사장을 멸시하셨도다
krv.LAM_2_7: 여호와께서 또 자기 제단을 버리시며 자기 성소를 미워하시며 궁장을 원수의 손에 붙이셨으매 저희가 여호와의 전에서 훤화하기를 절기날과 같이 하였도다
krv.LAM_2_8: 여호와께서 처녀 시온의 성을 헐기로 결심하시고 줄을 띠고 훼파함에서 손을 거두지 아니하사 성과 곽으로 통곡하게 하셨으매 저희가 함께 쇠하였도다
krv.LAM_2_9: 성문이 땅에 묻히며 빗장이 꺾여 훼파되고 왕과 방백들이 율법없는 열방 가운데 있으며 그 선지자들은 여호와의 묵시를 받지 못하는도다
krv.LAM_2_10: 처녀 시온의 장로들이 땅에 앉아 잠잠하고 티끌을 머리에 무릅쓰고 굵은 베를 허리에 둘렀음이여 예루살렘 처녀들은 머리를 땅에 숙였도다
krv.LAM_2_11: 내 눈이 눈물에 상하며 내 창자가 끓으며 내 간이 땅에 쏟아졌으니 이는 처녀 내 백성이 패망하여 어린 자녀와 젖먹는 아이들이 성읍 길거리에 혼미함이로다
krv.LAM_2_12: 저희가 성읍 길거리에서 상한 자처럼 혼미하여 그 어미의 품에서 혼이 떠날 때에 어미에게 이르기를 곡식과 포도주가 어디 있느뇨 하도다
krv.LAM_2_13: 처녀 예루살렘이여 내가 무엇으로 네게 증거하며 무엇으로 네게 비유할꼬 처녀 시온이여 내가 무엇으로 네게 비교하여 너를 위로할꼬 너의 파괴됨이 바다 같이 크니 누가 너를 고칠소냐
krv.LAM_2_14: 네 선지자들이 네게 대하여 헛되고 어리석은 묵시를 보았으므로 네 죄악을 드러내어서 네 사로잡힌 것을 돌이키지 못하였도다 저희가 거짓 경고와 미혹케 할 것만 보았도다
krv.LAM_2_15: 무릇 지나가는 자는 다 너를 향하여 박장하며 처녀 예루살렘을 향하여 비소하고 머리를 흔들며 말하기를 온전한 영광이라, 천하의 희락이라 일컫던 성이 이 성이냐 하며
krv.LAM_2_16: 너의 모든 원수는 너를 향하여 입을 벌리며 비소하고 이를 갈며 말하기를 우리가 저를 삼켰도다 우리가 바라던 날이 과연 이 날이라 우리가 얻기도 하고 보기도 하였다 하도다
krv.LAM_2_17: 여호와께서 이미 정하신 일을 행하시고 옛날에 명하신 말씀을 다 이루셨음이여 긍휼히 여기지 아니하시고 훼파하사 원수로 너를 인하여 즐거워하게 하며 너의 대적의 뿔로 높이 들리게 하셨도다
krv.LAM_2_18: 저희 마음이 주를 향하여 부르짖기를 처녀 시온의 성곽아 너는 밤낮으로 눈물을 강처럼 흘릴지어다 스스로 쉬지 말고 네 눈동자로 쉬게 하지 말지어다
krv.LAM_2_19: 밤 초경에 일어나 부르짖을지어다 네 마음을 주의 얼굴 앞에 물 쏟듯 할지어다 각 길머리에서 주려 혼미한 네 어린 자녀의 생명을 위하여 주를 향하여 손을 들지어다 하였도다
krv.LAM_2_20: 여호와여 감찰하소서 뉘게 이같이 행하셨는지요 여인들이 어찌 자기 열매 곧 손에 받든 아이를 먹으오며 제사장들과 선지자들이 어찌 주의 성소에서 살륙을 당하오리이까
krv.LAM_2_21: 노유는 다 길바닥에 엎드러졌사오며 내 처녀들과 소년들이 칼에 죽었나이다 주께서 진노하신 날에 죽이시되 긍휼히 여기지 아니하시고 살륙하셨나이다
krv.LAM_2_22: 주께서 내 두려운 일을 사방에서 부르시기를 절기에 무리를 부름 같이 하셨나이다 여호와께서 진노하신 날에 피하거나 남은 자가 없었나이다 내 손에 받들어 기르는 자를 내 원수가 다 멸하였나이다
krv.LAM_3_1: 여호와의 노하신 매로 인하여 고난 당한 자는 내로다
krv.LAM_3_2: 나를 이끌어 흑암에 행하고 광명에 행치 않게 하셨으며
krv.LAM_3_3: 종일토록 손을 돌이켜 자주 자주 나를 치시도다
krv.LAM_3_4: 나의 살과 가죽을 쇠하게 하시며 나의 뼈를 꺾으셨고
krv.LAM_3_6: 나로 흑암에 거하게 하시기를 죽은지 오랜 자 같게 하셨도다
krv.LAM_3_7: 나를 둘러 싸서 나가지 못하게 하시고 나의 사슬을 무겁게 하셨으며
krv.LAM_3_8: 내가 부르짖어 도움을 구하나 내 기도를 물리치시며
krv.LAM_3_9: 다듬은 돌을 쌓아 내 길을 막으사 내 첩경을 굽게 하셨도다
krv.LAM_3_10: 저는 내게 대하여 엎드리어 기다리는 곰과 은밀한 곳의 사자 같으사
krv.LAM_3_11: 나의 길로 치우치게 하시며 내 몸을 찢으시며 나로 적막하게 하셨도다
krv.LAM_3_14: 나는 내 모든 백성에게 조롱거리 곧 종일토록 그들의 노랫거리가 되었도다
krv.LAM_3_15: 나를 쓴 것으로 배불리시고 쑥으로 취하게 하셨으며
krv.LAM_3_16: 조약돌로 내 이를 꺾으시고 재로 나를 덮으셨도다
krv.LAM_3_17: 주께서 내 심령으로 평강을 멀리 떠나게 하시니 내가 복을 잊어버렸음이여
krv.LAM_3_18: 스스로 이르기를 나의 힘과 여호와께 대한 내 소망이 끊어졌다 하였도다
krv.LAM_3_19: 내 고초와 재난 곧 쑥과 담즙을 기억하소서
krv.LAM_3_20: 내 심령이 그것을 기억하고 낙심이 되오나
krv.LAM_3_22: 여호와의 자비와 긍휼이 무궁하시므로 우리가 진멸되지 아니함이니이다
krv.LAM_3_23: 이것이 아침마다 새로우니 주의 성실이 크도소이다
krv.LAM_3_24: 내 심령에 이르기를 여호와는 나의 기업이시니 그러므로 내가 저를 바라리라 하도다
krv.LAM_3_25: 무릇 기다리는 자에게나 구하는 영혼에게 여호와께서 선을 베푸시는도다
krv.LAM_3_26: 사람이 여호와의 구원을 바라고 잠잠히 기다림이 좋도다
krv.LAM_3_27: 사람이 젊었을 때에 멍에를 메는 것이 좋으니
krv.LAM_3_28: 혼자 앉아서 잠잠할 것은 주께서 그것을 메우셨음이라
krv.LAM_3_29: 입을 티끌에 댈지어다 혹시 소망이 있을지로다
krv.LAM_3_30: 때리는 자에게 뺨을 향하여 수욕으로 배불릴지어다
krv.LAM_3_31: 이는 주께서 영원토록 버리지 않으실 것임이며
krv.LAM_3_32: 저가 비록 근심케 하시나 그 풍부한 자비대로 긍휼히 여기실 것임이니라
krv.LAM_3_33: 주께서 인생으로 고생하며 근심하게 하심이 본심이 아니시로다
krv.LAM_3_34: 세상에 모든 갇힌 자를 발로 밟는 것과
krv.LAM_3_35: 지극히 높으신 자의 얼굴 앞에서 사람의 재판을 굽게 하는 것과
krv.LAM_3_36: 사람의 송사를 억울케 하는 것은 다 주의 기쁘게 보시는 것이 아니로다
krv.LAM_3_37: 주의 명령이 아니면 누가 능히 말하여 이루게 하라
krv.LAM_3_38: 화, 복이 지극히 높으신 자의 입으로 나오지 아니하느냐
krv.LAM_3_39: 살아 있는 사람은 자기 죄로 벌을 받나니 어찌 원망하랴
krv.LAM_3_40: 우리가 스스로 행위를 조사하고 여호와께로 돌아가자
krv.LAM_3_41: 마음과 손을 아울러 하늘에 계신 하나님께 들자
krv.LAM_3_42: 우리의 범죄함과 패역함을 주께서 사하지 아니하시고
krv.LAM_3_43: 진노로 스스로 가리우시고 우리를 군축하시며 살륙하사 긍휼을 베풀지 아니하셨나이다
krv.LAM_3_44: 주께서 구름으로 스스로 가리우사 기도로 상달치 못하게 하시고
krv.LAM_3_45: 우리를 열방 가운데서 진개와 폐물을 삼으셨으므로
krv.LAM_3_46: 우리의 모든 대적이 우리를 향하여 입을 크게 벌렸나이다
krv.LAM_3_47: 두려움과 함정과 잔해와 멸망이 우리에게 임하였도다
krv.LAM_3_48: 처녀 내 백성의 파멸을 인하여 내 눈에 눈물이 시내처럼 흐르도다
krv.LAM_3_49: 내 눈의 흐르는 눈물이 그치지 아니하고 쉬지 아니함이여
krv.LAM_3_51: 나의 성읍의 모든 여자를 인하여 내 눈이 내 심령을 상하게 하는도다
krv.LAM_3_52: 무고히 나의 대적이 된 자가 나를 새와 같이 심히 쫓도다
krv.LAM_3_53: 저희가 내 생명을 끊으려고 나를 구덩이에 넣고 그 위에 돌을 던짐이여
krv.LAM_3_54: 물이 내 머리에 넘치니 내가 스스로 이르기를 이제는 멸절되었다 하도다
krv.LAM_3_55: 여호와여 내가 심히 깊은 구덩이에서 주의 이름을 불렀나이다
krv.LAM_3_56: 주께서 이미 나의 음성을 들으셨사오니 이제 나의 탄식과 부르짖음에 주의 귀를 가리우지 마옵소서
krv.LAM_3_57: 내가 주께 아뢴 날에 주께서 내게 가까이 하여 가라사대 두려워 말라 하셨나이다
krv.LAM_3_58: 주여 주께서 내 심령의 원통을 펴셨고 내 생명을 속하셨나이다
krv.LAM_3_59: 여호와여 나의 억울을 감찰하셨사오니 나를 위하여 신원하옵소서
krv.LAM_3_60: 저희가 내게 보수하며 나를 모해함을 주께서 다 감찰하셨나이다
krv.LAM_3_61: 여호와여 저희가 나를 훼파하며 나를 모해하는 것
krv.LAM_3_62: 곧 일어나 나를 치는 자의 입술에서 나오는 것과 종일 모해하는 것을 들으셨나이다
krv.LAM_3_63: 저희가 앉든지 서든지 나를 노래하는 것을 주여 보옵소서
krv.LAM_3_64: 여호와여 주께서 저의 손으로 행한 대로 보응하사
krv.LAM_3_65: 그 마음을 강퍅하게 하시고 저주를 더하시며
krv.LAM_3_66: 진노로 저희를 군축하사 여호와의 천하에서 멸하시리이다
krv.LAM_4_1: 슬프다 어찌 그리 금이 빛을 잃고 정금이 변하였으며 성소의 돌이 각 거리 머리에 쏟아졌는고
krv.LAM_4_2: 시온의 아들들이 보배로워 정금에 비할러니 어찌 그리 토기장이의 만든 질항아리 같이 여김이 되었는고
krv.LAM_4_3: 들개는 오히려 젖을 내어 새끼를 먹이나 처녀 내 백성은 잔인하여 광야의 타조 같도다
krv.LAM_4_4: 젖먹이가 목말라서 혀가 입천장에 붙음이여 어린 아이가 떡을 구하나 떼어 줄 사람이 없도다
krv.LAM_4_5: 진수를 먹던 자가 거리에 외로움이여 전에는 붉은 옷을 입고 길리운 자가 이제는 거름더미를 안았도다
krv.LAM_4_6: 전에 소돔이 사람의 손을 대지 않고 경각간에 무너지더니 이제 처녀 내 백성의 죄가 소돔의 죄악보다 중하도다
krv.LAM_4_7: 전에는 존귀한 자의 몸이 눈보다 깨끗하고 젖보다 희며 산호보다 붉어 그 윤택함이 마광한 청옥 같더니
krv.LAM_4_8: 이제는 그 얼굴이 숯보다 검고 그 가죽이 뼈에 붙어 막대기 같이 말랐으니 거리에서 알 사람이 없도다
krv.LAM_4_9: 칼에 죽은 자가 주려 죽은 자보다 나음은 토지 소산이 끊어지므로 이들이 찔림 같이 점점 쇠약하여 감이로다
krv.LAM_4_10: 처녀 내 백성의 멸망할 때에 자비한 부녀가 손으로 자기 자녀를 삶아 식물을 삼았도다
krv.LAM_4_11: 여호와께서 분을 발하시며 맹렬한 노를 쏟으심이여 시온에 불을 피우사 그 지대를 사르셨도다
krv.LAM_4_12: 대적과 원수가 예루살렘 성문으로 들어갈 줄은 세상 열왕과 천하 모든 백성이 믿지 못하였었도다
krv.LAM_4_13: 그 선지자들의 죄와 제사장들의 죄악을 인함이니 저희가 성읍 중에서 의인의 피를 흘렀도다
krv.LAM_4_14: 저희가 거리에서 소경 같이 방황함이여 그 옷이 피에 더러웠으므로 사람이 만질 수 없도다
krv.LAM_4_15: 사람이 저희에게 외쳐 이르기를 부정하다 가라, 가라, 가라, 만지지말라 하였음이여 저희가 도망하여 방황할 때에 이방인이 이르기를 저희가 다시는 여기 거하지 못하리라 하였도다
krv.LAM_4_16: 여호와께서 노하여 흩으시고 다시 권고치 아니하시리니 저희가 제사장들을 높이지 아니하였으며 장로들을 대접지 아니하였음이로다
krv.LAM_4_17: 우리가 헛되이 도움을 바라므로 우리 눈이 상함이여 우리를 구원치 못할 나라를 바라보고 바라보았도다
krv.LAM_4_18: 저희가 우리 자취를 엿보니 우리가 거리에 행할 수 없음이여 우리의 끝이 가깝고 우리의 날이 다하였고 우리의 마지막이 이르렀도다
krv.LAM_4_19: 우리를 쫓는 자가 공중의 독수리보다 빠름이여 산꼭대기에서도 쫓고 광야에도 매복하였도다
krv.LAM_4_20: 우리의 콧김 곧 여호와의 기름 부으신 자가 저희 함정에 빠졌음이여 우리가 저를 가리키며 전에 이르기를 우리가 저의 그늘 아래서 열국 중에 살겠다 하던 자로다
krv.LAM_4_21: 우스 땅에 거하는 처녀 에돔아 즐거워하며 기뻐하려무나 잔이 네게도 이를지니 네가 취하여 벌거벗으리라
krv.LAM_4_22: 처녀 시온아 네 죄악의 형벌이 다하였으니 주께서 다시는 너로 사로잡혀 가지 않게 하시리로다 처녀 에돔아 주께서 네 죄악을 벌하시며 네 허물을 드러내시리로다
krv.LAM_5_1: 여호와여 우리의 당한 것을 기억하시고 우리의 수욕을 감찰하옵소서
krv.LAM_5_2: 우리 기업이 외인에게, 우리 집들도 외인에게 돌아갔나이다
krv.LAM_5_3: 우리는 아비 없는 외로운 자식이오며 우리 어미는 과부 같으니
krv.LAM_5_4: 우리가 은을 주고 물을 마시며 값을 주고 섶을 얻으오며
krv.LAM_5_5: 우리를 쫓는 자는 우리 목을 눌렀사오니 우리가 곤비하여 쉴 수 없나이다
krv.LAM_5_6: 우리가 애굽 사람과 앗수르 사람과 악수하고 양식을 얻어 배불리고자 하였나이다
krv.LAM_5_7: 우리 열조는 범죄하고 없어졌고 우리는 그 죄악을 담당하였나이다
krv.LAM_5_8: 종들이 우리를 관할함이여 그 손에서 건져낼 자가 없나이다
krv.LAM_5_9: 광야에는 칼이 있으므로 죽기를 무릅써야 양식을 얻사오니
krv.LAM_5_10: 주림의 열기로 인하여 우리의 피부가 아궁이처럼 검으니이다
krv.LAM_5_11: 대적이 시온에서 부녀들을, 유다 각 성에서 처녀들을 욕보였나이다
krv.LAM_5_12: 방백들의 손이 매어달리며 장로들의 얼굴이 존경을 받지 못하나이다
krv.LAM_5_13: 소년들이 맷돌을 지오며 아이들이 섶을 지다가 엎드러지오며
krv.LAM_5_14: 노인은 다시 성문에 앉지 못하며 소년은 다시 노래하지 못하나이다
krv.LAM_5_15: 우리 마음에 희락이 그쳤고 우리의 무도가 변하여 애통이 되었사오며
krv.LAM_5_16: 우리 머리에서 면류관이 떨어졌사오니 오호라 우리의 범죄함을 인함이니이다
krv.LAM_5_17: 이러므로 우리 마음이 피곤하고 이러므로 우리 눈이 어두우며
krv.LAM_5_18: 시온 산이 황무하여 여우가 거기서 노나이다
krv.LAM_5_19: 여호와여 주는 영원히 계시오며 주의 보좌는 세세에 미치나이다
krv.LAM_5_20: 주께서 어찌하여 우리를 영원히 잊으시오며 우리를 이같이 오래 버리시나이까
krv.LAM_5_21: 여호와여 우리를 주께로 돌이키소서 그리하시면 우리가 주께로 돌아 가겠사오니 우리의 날을 다시 새롭게 하사 옛적 같게 하옵소서
krv.LAM_5_22: 주께서 우리를 아주 버리셨사오며 우리에게 진노하심이 특심하시니이다

kjv.LAM_1_1: How doth the city sit solitary, that was full of people! how is she become as a widow! she that was great among the nations, and princess among the provinces, how is she become tributary!
kjv.LAM_1_2: She weepeth sore in the night, and her tears are on her cheeks: among all her lovers she hath none to comfort her: all her friends have dealt treacherously with her, they are become her enemies.
kjv.LAM_1_3: Judah is gone into captivity because of affliction, and because of great servitude: she dwelleth among the heathen, she findeth no rest: all her persecutors overtook her between the straits.
kjv.LAM_1_4: The ways of Zion do mourn, because none come to the solemn feasts: all her gates are desolate: her priests sigh, her virgins are afflicted, and she is in bitterness.
kjv.LAM_1_5: Her adversaries are the chief, her enemies prosper; for the Lord hath afflicted her for the multitude of her transgressions: her children are gone into captivity before the enemy.
kjv.LAM_1_6: And from the daughter of Zion all her beauty is departed: her princes are become like harts that find no pasture, and they are gone without strength before the pursuer.
kjv.LAM_1_7: Jerusalem remembered in the days of her affliction and of her miseries all her pleasant things that she had in the days of old, when her people fell into the hand of the enemy, and none did help her: the adversaries saw her, and did mock at her sabbaths.
kjv.LAM_1_8: Jerusalem hath grievously sinned; therefore she is removed: all that honoured her despise her, because they have seen her nakedness: yea, she sigheth, and turneth backward.
kjv.LAM_1_9: Her filthiness is in her skirts; she remembereth not her last end; therefore she came down wonderfully: she had no comforter. O Lord, behold my affliction: for the enemy hath magnified himself.
kjv.LAM_1_10: The adversary hath spread out his hand upon all her pleasant things: for she hath seen that the heathen entered into her sanctuary, whom thou didst command that they should not enter into thy congregation.
kjv.LAM_1_11: All her people sigh, they seek bread; they have given their pleasant things for meat to relieve the soul: see, O Lord, and consider; for I am become vile.
kjv.LAM_1_12: Is it nothing to you, all ye that pass by? behold, and see if there be any sorrow like unto my sorrow, which is done unto me, wherewith the Lord hath afflicted me in the day of his fierce anger.
kjv.LAM_1_13: From above hath he sent fire into my bones, and it prevaileth against them: he hath spread a net for my feet, he hath turned me back: he hath made me desolate and faint all the day.
kjv.LAM_1_14: The yoke of my transgressions is bound by his hand: they are wreathed, and come up upon my neck: he hath made my strength to fall, the Lord hath delivered me into their hands, from whom I am not able to rise up.
kjv.LAM_1_15: The Lord hath trodden under foot all my mighty men in the midst of me: he hath called an assembly against me to crush my young men: the Lord hath trodden the virgin, the daughter of Judah, as in a winepress.
kjv.LAM_1_16: For these things I weep; mine eye, mine eye runneth down with water, because the comforter that should relieve my soul is far from me: my children are desolate, because the enemy prevailed.
kjv.LAM_1_17: Zion spreadeth forth her hands, and there is none to comfort her: the Lord hath commanded concerning Jacob, that his adversaries should be round about him: Jerusalem is as a menstruous woman among them.
kjv.LAM_1_18: The Lord is righteous; for I have rebelled against his commandment: hear, I pray you, all people, and behold my sorrow: my virgins and my young men are gone into captivity.
kjv.LAM_1_19: I called for my lovers, but they deceived me: my priests and mine elders gave up the ghost in the city, while they sought their meat to relieve their souls.
kjv.LAM_1_20: Behold, O Lord; for I am in distress: my bowels are troubled; mine heart is turned within me; for I have grievously rebelled: abroad the sword bereaveth, at home there is as death.
kjv.LAM_1_21: They have heard that I sigh: there is none to comfort me: all mine enemies have heard of my trouble; they are glad that thou hast done it: thou wilt bring the day that thou hast called, and they shall be like unto me.
kjv.LAM_1_22: Let all their wickedness come before thee; and do unto them, as thou hast done unto me for all my transgressions: for my sighs are many, and my heart is faint.
kjv.LAM_2_1: How hath the Lord covered the daughter of Zion with a cloud in his anger, and cast down from heaven unto the earth the beauty of Israel, and remembered not his footstool in the day of his anger!
kjv.LAM_2_2: The Lord hath swallowed up all the habitations of Jacob, and hath not pitied: he hath thrown down in his wrath the strong holds of the daughter of Judah; he hath brought them down to the ground: he hath polluted the kingdom and the princes thereof.
kjv.LAM_2_3: He hath cut off in his fierce anger all the horn of Israel: he hath drawn back his right hand from before the enemy, and he burned against Jacob like a flaming fire, which devoureth round about.
kjv.LAM_2_4: He hath bent his bow like an enemy: he stood with his right hand as an adversary, and slew all that were pleasant to the eye in the tabernacle of the daughter of Zion: he poured out his fury like fire.
kjv.LAM_2_5: The Lord was as an enemy: he hath swallowed up Israel, he hath swallowed up all her palaces: he hath destroyed his strong holds, and hath increased in the daughter of Judah mourning and lamentation.
kjv.LAM_2_6: And he hath violently taken away his tabernacle, as if it were of a garden: he hath destroyed his places of the assembly: the Lord hath caused the solemn feasts and sabbaths to be forgotten in Zion, and hath despised in the indignation of his anger the king and the priest.
kjv.LAM_2_7: The Lord hath cast off his altar, he hath abhorred his sanctuary, he hath given up into the hand of the enemy the walls of her palaces; they have made a noise in the house of the Lord, as in the day of a solemn feast.
kjv.LAM_2_8: The Lord hath purposed to destroy the wall of the daughter of Zion: he hath stretched out a line, he hath not withdrawn his hand from destroying: therefore he made the rampart and the wall to lament; they languished together.
kjv.LAM_2_9: Her gates are sunk into the ground; he hath destroyed and broken her bars: her king and her princes are among the Gentiles: the law is no more; her prophets also find no vision from the Lord.
kjv.LAM_2_10: The elders of the daughter of Zion sit upon the ground, and keep silence: they have cast up dust upon their heads; they have girded themselves with sackcloth: the virgins of Jerusalem hang down their heads to the ground.
kjv.LAM_2_11: Mine eyes do fail with tears, my bowels are troubled, my liver is poured upon the earth, for the destruction of the daughter of my people; because the children and the sucklings swoon in the streets of the city.
kjv.LAM_2_12: They say to their mothers, Where is corn and wine? when they swooned as the wounded in the streets of the city, when their soul was poured out into their mothers' bosom.
kjv.LAM_2_13: What thing shall I take to witness for thee? what thing shall I liken to thee, O daughter of Jerusalem? what shall I equal to thee, that I may comfort thee, O virgin daughter of Zion? for thy breach is great like the sea: who can heal thee?
kjv.LAM_2_14: Thy prophets have seen vain and foolish things for thee: and they have not discovered thine iniquity, to turn away thy captivity; but have seen for thee false burdens and causes of banishment.
kjv.LAM_2_15: All that pass by clap their hands at thee; they hiss and wag their head at the daughter of Jerusalem, saying, Is this the city that men call The perfection of beauty, The joy of the whole earth?
kjv.LAM_2_16: All thine enemies have opened their mouth against thee: they hiss and gnash the teeth: they say, We have swallowed her up: certainly this is the day that we looked for; we have found, we have seen it.
kjv.LAM_2_17: The Lord hath done that which he had devised; he hath fulfilled his word that he had commanded in the days of old: he hath thrown down, and hath not pitied: and he hath caused thine enemy to rejoice over thee, he hath set up the horn of thine adversaries.
kjv.LAM_2_18: Their heart cried unto the Lord, O wall of the daughter of Zion, let tears run down like a river day and night: give thyself no rest; let not the apple of thine eye cease.
kjv.LAM_2_19: Arise, cry out in the night: in the beginning of the watches pour out thine heart like water before the face of the Lord: lift up thy hands toward him for the life of thy young children, that faint for hunger in the top of every street.
kjv.LAM_2_20: Behold, O Lord, and consider to whom thou hast done this. Shall the women eat their fruit, and children of a span long? shall the priest and the prophet be slain in the sanctuary of the Lord?
kjv.LAM_2_21: The young and the old lie on the ground in the streets: my virgins and my young men are fallen by the sword; thou hast slain them in the day of thine anger; thou hast killed, and not pitied.
kjv.LAM_2_22: Thou hast called as in a solemn day my terrors round about, so that in the day of the Lord's anger none escaped nor remained: those that I have swaddled and brought up hath mine enemy consumed.
kjv.LAM_3_1: I Am the man that hath seen affliction by the rod of his wrath.
kjv.LAM_3_2: He hath led me, and brought me into darkness, but not into light.
kjv.LAM_3_3: Surely against me is he turned; he turneth his hand against me all the day.
kjv.LAM_3_4: My flesh and my skin hath he made old; he hath broken my bones.
kjv.LAM_3_5: He hath builded against me, and compassed me with gall and travail.
kjv.LAM_3_6: He hath set me in dark places, as they that be dead of old.
kjv.LAM_3_7: He hath hedged me about, that I cannot get out: he hath made my chain heavy.
kjv.LAM_3_8: Also when I cry and shout, he shutteth out my prayer.
kjv.LAM_3_9: He hath inclosed my ways with hewn stone, he hath made my paths crooked.
kjv.LAM_3_10: He was unto me as a bear lying in wait, and as a lion in secret places.
kjv.LAM_3_11: He hath turned aside my ways, and pulled me in pieces: he hath made me desolate.
kjv.LAM_3_12: He hath bent his bow, and set me as a mark for the arrow.
kjv.LAM_3_13: He hath caused the arrows of his quiver to enter into my reins.
kjv.LAM_3_14: I was a derision to all my people; and their song all the day.
kjv.LAM_3_15: He hath filled me with bitterness, he hath made me drunken with wormwood.
kjv.LAM_3_16: He hath also broken my teeth with gravel stones, he hath covered me with ashes.
kjv.LAM_3_17: And thou hast removed my soul far off from peace: I forgat prosperity.
kjv.LAM_3_18: And I said, My strength and my hope is perished from the Lord:
kjv.LAM_3_19: Remembering mine affliction and my misery, the wormwood and the gall.
kjv.LAM_3_20: My soul hath them still in remembrance, and is humbled in me.
kjv.LAM_3_21: This I recall to my mind, therefore have I hope.
kjv.LAM_3_22: It is of the Lord's mercies that we are not consumed, because his compassions fail not.
kjv.LAM_3_23: They are new every morning: great is thy faithfulness.
kjv.LAM_3_24: The Lord is my portion, saith my soul; therefore will I hope in him.
kjv.LAM_3_25: The Lord is good unto them that wait for him, to the soul that seeketh him.
kjv.LAM_3_26: It is good that a man should both hope and quietly wait for the salvation of the Lord.
kjv.LAM_3_27: It is good for a man that he bear the yoke in his youth.
kjv.LAM_3_28: He sitteth alone and keepeth silence, because he hath borne it upon him.
kjv.LAM_3_29: He putteth his mouth in the dust; if so be there may be hope.
kjv.LAM_3_30: He giveth his cheek to him that smiteth him: he is filled full with reproach.
kjv.LAM_3_31: For the Lord will not cast off for ever:
kjv.LAM_3_32: But though he cause grief, yet will he have compassion according to the multitude of his mercies.
kjv.LAM_3_33: For he doth not afflict willingly nor grieve the children of men.
kjv.LAM_3_34: To crush under his feet all the prisoners of the earth.
kjv.LAM_3_35: To turn aside the right of a man before the face of the most High,
kjv.LAM_3_36: To subvert a man in his cause, the Lord approveth not.
kjv.LAM_3_37: Who is he that saith, and it cometh to pass, when the Lord commandeth it not?
kjv.LAM_3_38: Out of the mouth of the most High proceedeth not evil and good?
kjv.LAM_3_39: Wherefore doth a living man complain, a man for the punishment of his sins?
kjv.LAM_3_40: Let us search and try our ways, and turn again to the Lord.
kjv.LAM_3_41: Let us lift up our heart with our hands unto God in the heavens.
kjv.LAM_3_42: We have transgressed and have rebelled: thou hast not pardoned.
kjv.LAM_3_43: Thou hast covered with anger, and persecuted us: thou hast slain, thou hast not pitied.
kjv.LAM_3_44: Thou hast covered thyself with a cloud, that our prayer should not pass through.
kjv.LAM_3_45: Thou hast made us as the offscouring and refuse in the midst of the people.
kjv.LAM_3_46: All our enemies have opened their mouths against us.
kjv.LAM_3_47: Fear and a snare is come upon us, desolation and destruction.
kjv.LAM_3_48: Mine eye runneth down with rivers of water for the destruction of the daughter of my people.
kjv.LAM_3_49: Mine eye trickleth down, and ceaseth not, without any intermission.
kjv.LAM_3_50: Till the Lord look down, and behold from heaven.
kjv.LAM_3_51: Mine eye affecteth mine heart because of all the daughters of my city.
kjv.LAM_3_52: Mine enemies chased me sore, like a bird, without cause.
kjv.LAM_3_53: They have cut off my life in the dungeon, and cast a stone upon me.
kjv.LAM_3_54: Waters flowed over mine head; then I said, I am cut off.
kjv.LAM_3_55: I called upon thy name, O Lord, out of the low dungeon.
kjv.LAM_3_56: Thou hast heard my voice: hide not thine ear at my breathing, at my cry.
kjv.LAM_3_57: Thou drewest near in the day that I called upon thee: thou saidst, Fear not.
kjv.LAM_3_58: O Lord, thou hast pleaded the causes of my soul; thou hast redeemed my life.
kjv.LAM_3_59: O Lord, thou hast seen my wrong: judge thou my cause.
kjv.LAM_3_60: Thou hast seen all their vengeance and all their imaginations against me.
kjv.LAM_3_61: Thou hast heard their reproach, O Lord, and all their imaginations against me;
kjv.LAM_3_62: The lips of those that rose up against me, and their device against me all the day.
kjv.LAM_3_63: Behold their sitting down, and their rising up; I am their musick.
kjv.LAM_3_64: Render unto them a recompence, O Lord, according to the work of their hands.
kjv.LAM_3_65: Give them sorrow of heart, thy curse unto them.
kjv.LAM_3_66: Persecute and destroy them in anger from under the heavens of the Lord.
kjv.LAM_4_1: How is the gold become dim! how is the most fine gold changed! the stones of the sanctuary are poured out in the top of every street.
kjv.LAM_4_2: The precious sons of Zion, comparable to fine gold, how are they esteemed as earthen pitchers, the work of the hands of the potter!
kjv.LAM_4_3: Even the sea monsters draw out the breast, they give suck to their young ones: the daughter of my people is become cruel, like the ostriches in the wilderness.
kjv.LAM_4_4: The tongue of the sucking child cleaveth to the roof of his mouth for thirst: the young children ask bread, and no man breaketh it unto them.
kjv.LAM_4_5: They that did feed delicately are desolate in the streets: they that were brought up in scarlet embrace dunghills.
kjv.LAM_4_6: For the punishment of the iniquity of the daughter of my people is greater than the punishment of the sin of Sodom, that was overthrown as in a moment, and no hands stayed on her.
kjv.LAM_4_7: Her Nazarites were purer than snow, they were whiter than milk, they were more ruddy in body than rubies, their polishing was of sapphire:
kjv.LAM_4_8: Their visage is blacker than a coal; they are not known in the streets: their skin cleaveth to their bones; it is withered, it is become like a stick.
kjv.LAM_4_9: They that be slain with the sword are better than they that be slain with hunger: for these pine away, stricken through for want of the fruits of the field.
kjv.LAM_4_10: The hands of the pitiful women have sodden their own children: they were their meat in the destruction of the daughter of my people.
kjv.LAM_4_11: The Lord hath accomplished his fury; he hath poured out his fierce anger, and hath kindled a fire in Zion, and it hath devoured the foundations thereof.
kjv.LAM_4_12: The kings of the earth, and all the inhabitants of the world, would not have believed that the adversary and the enemy should have entered into the gates of Jerusalem.
kjv.LAM_4_13: For the sins of her prophets, and the iniquities of her priests, that have shed the blood of the just in the midst of her,
kjv.LAM_4_14: They have wandered as blind men in the streets, they have polluted themselves with blood, so that men could not touch their garments.
kjv.LAM_4_15: They cried unto them, Depart ye; it is unclean; depart, depart, touch not: when they fled away and wandered, they said among the heathen, They shall no more sojourn there.
kjv.LAM_4_16: The anger of the Lord hath divided them; he will no more regard them: they respected not the persons of the priests, they favoured not the elders.
kjv.LAM_4_17: As for us, our eyes as yet failed for our vain help: in our watching we have watched for a nation that could not save us.
kjv.LAM_4_18: They hunt our steps, that we cannot go in our streets: our end is near, our days are fulfilled; for our end is come.
kjv.LAM_4_19: Our persecutors are swifter than the eagles of the heaven: they pursued us upon the mountains, they laid wait for us in the wilderness.
kjv.LAM_4_20: The breath of our nostrils, the anointed of the Lord, was taken in their pits, of whom we said, Under his shadow we shall live among the heathen.
kjv.LAM_4_21: Rejoice and be glad, O daughter of Edom, that dwellest in the land of Uz; the cup also shall pass through unto thee: thou shalt be drunken, and shalt make thyself naked.
kjv.LAM_4_22: The punishment of thine iniquity is accomplished, O daughter of Zion; he will no more carry thee away into captivity: he will visit thine iniquity, O daughter of Edom; he will discover thy sins.
kjv.LAM_5_1: Remember, O Lord, what is come upon us: consider, and behold our reproach.
kjv.LAM_5_2: Our inheritance is turned to strangers, our houses to aliens.
kjv.LAM_5_3: We are orphans and fatherless, our mothers are as widows.
kjv.LAM_5_4: We have drunken our water for money; our wood is sold unto us.
kjv.LAM_5_5: Our necks are under persecution: we labour, and have no rest.
kjv.LAM_5_6: We have given the hand to the Egyptians, and to the Assyrians, to be satisfied with bread.
kjv.LAM_5_7: Our fathers have sinned, and are not; and we have borne their iniquities.
kjv.LAM_5_8: Servants have ruled over us: there is none that doth deliver us out of their hand.
kjv.LAM_5_9: We gat our bread with the peril of our lives because of the sword of the wilderness.
kjv.LAM_5_10: Our skin was black like an oven because of the terrible famine.
kjv.LAM_5_11: They ravished the women in Zion, and the maids in the cities of Judah.
kjv.LAM_5_12: Princes are hanged up by their hand: the faces of elders were not honoured.
kjv.LAM_5_13: They took the young men to grind, and the children fell under the wood.
kjv.LAM_5_14: The elders have ceased from the gate, the young men from their musick.
kjv.LAM_5_15: The joy of our heart is ceased; our dance is turned into mourning.
kjv.LAM_5_16: The crown is fallen from our head: woe unto us, that we have sinned!
kjv.LAM_5_17: For this our heart is faint; for these things our eyes are dim.
kjv.LAM_5_18: Because of the mountain of Zion, which is desolate, the foxes walk upon it.
kjv.LAM_5_19: Thou, O Lord, remainest for ever; thy throne from generation to generation.
kjv.LAM_5_20: Wherefore dost thou forget us for ever, and forsake us so long time?
kjv.LAM_5_21: Turn thou us unto thee, O Lord, and we shall be turned; renew our days as of old.
kjv.LAM_5_22: But thou hast utterly rejected us; thou art very wroth against us.

net.LAM_1_1: Alas! The city once full of people now sits all alone! The prominent lady among the nations has become a widow! The princess who once ruled the provinces has become a forced laborer! ב (Bet)
net.LAM_1_2: She weeps bitterly at night; tears stream down her cheeks. She has no one to comfort her among all her lovers. All her friends have betrayed her; they have become her enemies. ג (Gimel)
net.LAM_1_3: Judah has departed into exile under affliction and harsh oppression. She lives among the nations; she has found no resting place. All who pursued her overtook her in narrow straits. ד (Dalet)
net.LAM_1_4: The roads to Zion mourn because no one travels to the festivals. All her city gates are deserted; her priests groan. Her virgins grieve; she is in bitter anguish! ה (He)
net.LAM_1_5: Her foes subjugated her; her enemies are at ease. For the Lord afflicted her because of her many acts of rebellion. Her children went away captive before the enemy. ו (Vav)
net.LAM_1_6: All of Daughter Zion’s splendor has departed. Her leaders became like deer; they found no pasture, so they were too exhausted to escape from the hunter. ז (Zayin)
net.LAM_1_7: Jerusalem remembers, when she became a poor homeless person, all her treasures that she owned in days of old. When her people fell into an enemy’s grip, none of her allies came to her rescue. Her enemies gloated over her; they sneered at her downfall. ח (Khet)
net.LAM_1_8: Jerusalem committed terrible sin; therefore she became an object of scorn. All who admired her have despised her because they have seen her nakedness. She groans aloud and turns away in shame. ט (Tet)
net.LAM_1_9: Her menstrual flow has soiled her clothing; she did not consider the consequences of her sin. Her demise was astonishing, and there was no one to comfort her. She cried, “Look, O Lord, on my affliction because my enemy boasts!” י (Yod)
net.LAM_1_10: An enemy grabbed all her valuables. Indeed she watched in horror as Gentiles invaded her holy temple — those whom you had commanded: “They must not enter your assembly place.” כ (Kaf)
net.LAM_1_11: All her people groaned as they searched for a morsel of bread. They exchanged their valuables for just enough food to stay alive. Jerusalem Speaks “Look, O Lord! Consider that I have become worthless!” ל (Lamed)
net.LAM_1_12: Is it nothing to you, all you who pass by on the road? Look and see! Is there any pain like mine? The Lord has afflicted me, he has inflicted it on me when he burned with anger. מ (Mem)
net.LAM_1_13: He sent down fire into my bones, and it overcame them. He spread out a trapper’s net for my feet; he made me turn back. He has made me desolate; I am faint all day long. נ (Nun)
net.LAM_1_14: My sins are bound around my neck like a yoke; they are fastened together by his hand. He has placed his yoke on my neck; he has sapped my strength. The Lord has handed me over to those whom I cannot resist. ס (Samek)
net.LAM_1_15: He rounded up all my mighty ones; The Lord did this in my midst. He summoned an assembly against me to shatter my young men. The Lord has stomped like grapes the virgin daughter, Judah. ע (Ayin)
net.LAM_1_16: I weep because of these things; my eyes flow with tears. For there is no one in sight who can comfort me or encourage me. My children are desolated because an enemy has prevailed. פ (Pe) The Prophet Speaks
net.LAM_1_17: Zion spread out her hands, but there is no one to comfort her. The Lord has issued a decree against Jacob; his neighbors have become his enemies. Jerusalem has become like filthy garbage in their midst. צ (Tsade) Jerusalem Speaks
net.LAM_1_18: The Lord is right to judge me! Yes, I rebelled against his commands. Please listen, all you nations, and look at my suffering! My young women and men have gone into exile. ק (Qof)
net.LAM_1_19: I called for my lovers, but they had deceived me. My priests and my elders perished in the city. Truly they had searched for food to keep themselves alive. ר (Resh)
net.LAM_1_20: Look, O Lord! I am distressed; my stomach is in knots! My heart is pounding inside me. Yes, I was terribly rebellious! Out in the street the sword bereaves a mother of her children; Inside the house death is present. ש (Sin/Shin)
net.LAM_1_21: They have heard that I groan, yet there is no one to comfort me. All my enemies have heard of my trouble; they are glad that you have brought it about. Bring about the day of judgment that you promised so that they may end up like me! ת (Tav)
net.LAM_1_22: Let all their wickedness come before you; afflict them just as you have afflicted me because of all my acts of rebellion. For my groans are many, and my heart is sick with sorrow.
net.LAM_2_1: Alas! The Lord has covered Daughter Zion with his anger. He has thrown down the splendor of Israel from heaven to earth; he did not protect his temple when he displayed his anger. ב (Bet)
net.LAM_2_2: The Lord destroyed mercilessly all the homes of Jacob’s descendants. In his anger he tore down the fortified cities of Daughter Judah. He knocked to the ground and humiliated the kingdom and its rulers. ג (Gimel)
net.LAM_2_3: In fierce anger he destroyed the whole army of Israel. He withdrew his right hand as the enemy attacked. He was like a raging fire in the land of Jacob; it consumed everything around it. ד (Dalet)
net.LAM_2_4: He prepared his bow like an enemy; his right hand was ready to shoot. Like a foe he killed everyone, even our strong young men; he has poured out his anger like fire on the tent of Daughter Zion. ה (He)
net.LAM_2_5: The Lord, like an enemy, destroyed Israel. He destroyed all her palaces; he ruined her fortified cities. He made everyone in Daughter Judah mourn and lament. ו (Vav)
net.LAM_2_6: He destroyed his temple as if it were a vineyard; he destroyed his appointed meeting place. The Lord has made those in Zion forget both the festivals and the Sabbaths. In his fierce anger he has spurned both king and priest. ז (Zayin)
net.LAM_2_7: The Lord rejected his altar and abhorred his temple. He handed over to the enemy Jerusalem’s palace walls; the enemy shouted in the Lord’s temple as if it were a feast day. ח (Khet)
net.LAM_2_8: The Lord was determined to tear down Daughter Zion’s wall. He prepared to knock it down; he did not withdraw his hand from destroying. He made the ramparts and fortified walls lament; together they mourned their ruin. ט (Tet)
net.LAM_2_9: Her city gates have fallen to the ground; he smashed to bits the bars that lock her gates. Her king and princes were taken into exile; there is no more guidance available. As for her prophets, they no longer receive a vision from the Lord. י (Yod)
net.LAM_2_10: The elders of Daughter Zion sit on the ground in silence. They have thrown dirt on their heads; They have dressed in sackcloth. Jerusalem’s young women stare down at the ground. כ (Kaf)
net.LAM_2_11: My eyes are worn out from weeping; my stomach is in knots. My heart is poured out on the ground due to the destruction of my helpless people; children and infants faint in the town squares. ל (Lamed)
net.LAM_2_12: Children say to their mothers, “Where are food and drink?” They faint like a wounded warrior in the city squares. They die slowly in their mothers’ arms. מ (Mem)
net.LAM_2_13: With what can I equate you? To what can I compare you, O Daughter Jerusalem? To what can I liken you so that I might comfort you, O Virgin Daughter Zion? Your wound is as deep as the sea. Who can heal you? נ (Nun)
net.LAM_2_14: Your prophets saw visions for you that were worthless whitewash. They failed to expose your sin so as to restore your fortunes. They saw oracles for you that were worthless lies. ס (Samek)
net.LAM_2_15: All who passed by on the road clapped their hands to mock you. They sneered and shook their heads at Daughter Jerusalem. “Ha! Is this the city they called ‘the perfection of beauty, the source of joy of the whole earth!’?” פ (Pe)
net.LAM_2_16: All your enemies gloated over you. They sneered and gnashed their teeth; they said, “We have destroyed her! Ha! We have waited a long time for this day. We have lived to see it!” ע (Ayin)
net.LAM_2_17: The Lord has done what he planned; he has fulfilled his promise that he threatened long ago: He has overthrown you without mercy and has enabled the enemy to gloat over you; he has exalted your adversaries’ power. צ (Tsade)
net.LAM_2_18: Cry out from your heart to the Lord, O wall of Daughter Zion! Make your tears flow like a river all day and all night long! Do not rest; do not let your tears stop! ק (Qof)
net.LAM_2_19: Get up! Cry out in the night when the night watches start! Pour out your heart like water before the face of the Lord! Lift up your hands to him for your children’s lives; they are fainting from hunger at every street corner. ר (Resh) Jerusalem Speaks
net.LAM_2_20: Look, O Lord! Consider! Whom have you ever afflicted like this? Should women eat their offspring, their healthy infants? Should priest and prophet be killed in the Lord’s sanctuary? ש (Sin/Shin)
net.LAM_2_21: The young boys and old men lie dead on the ground in the streets. My young women and my young men have fallen by the sword. You killed them when you were angry; you slaughtered them without mercy. ת (Tav)
net.LAM_2_22: As if it were a feast day, you call enemies to terrify me on every side. On the day of the Lord’s anger no one escaped or survived. My enemy has finished off those healthy infants whom I bore and raised.
net.LAM_3_1: I am the man who has experienced affliction from the rod of theLord’s wrath.
net.LAM_3_2: He drove me into captivity and made me walk in darkness and not light.
net.LAM_3_3: He repeatedly attacks me; he turns his hand against me all day long. ב (Bet)
net.LAM_3_4: He has made my mortal skin waste away; he has broken my bones.
net.LAM_3_5: He has besieged and surrounded me with bitter hardship.
net.LAM_3_6: He has made me reside in deepest darkness like those who died long ago. ג (Gimel)
net.LAM_3_7: He has walled me in so that I cannot get out; he has weighted me down with heavy prison chains.
net.LAM_3_8: Also, when I cry out desperately for help, he has shut out my prayer.
net.LAM_3_9: He has blocked every road I take with a wall of hewn stones; he has made every path impassable. ד (Dalet)
net.LAM_3_10: To me he is like a bear lying in ambush, like a hidden lion stalking its prey.
net.LAM_3_11: He has obstructed my paths and torn me to pieces; he has made me desolate.
net.LAM_3_12: He drew his bow and made me the target for his arrow. ה (He)
net.LAM_3_13: He shot his arrows into my heart.
net.LAM_3_14: I have become the laughingstock of all people, their mocking song all day long.
net.LAM_3_15: He has given me my fill of bitter herbs and made me drunk with bitterness. ו (Vav)
net.LAM_3_16: He ground my teeth in gravel; he trampled me in the dust.
net.LAM_3_17: I am deprived of peace; I have forgotten what happiness is.
net.LAM_3_18: So I said, “My endurance has expired; I have lost all hope of deliverance from the Lord.” ז (Zayin)
net.LAM_3_19: Remember my impoverished and homeless condition, which is a bitter poison.
net.LAM_3_20: I continually think about this, and I am depressed.
net.LAM_3_21: But this I call to mind; therefore I have hope: ח (Khet)
net.LAM_3_22: The Lord’s loyal kindness never ceases; his compassions never end.
net.LAM_3_23: They are fresh every morning; your faithfulness is abundant!
net.LAM_3_24: “My portion is the Lord,” I have said to myself, so I will put my hope in him. ט (Tet)
net.LAM_3_25: The Lord is good to those who trust in him, to the one who seeks him.
net.LAM_3_26: It is good to wait patiently for deliverance from the Lord.
net.LAM_3_27: It is good for a man to bear the yoke while he is young. י (Yod)
net.LAM_3_28: Let a person sit alone in silence, when the Lord is disciplining him.
net.LAM_3_29: Let him bury his face in the dust; perhaps there is hope.
net.LAM_3_30: Let him offer his cheek to the one who hits him; let him have his fill of insults. כ (Kaf)
net.LAM_3_31: For the Lord will not reject us forever.
net.LAM_3_32: Though he causes us grief, he then has compassion on us according to the abundance of his loyal kindness.
net.LAM_3_33: For he is not predisposed to afflict or to grieve people. ל (Lamed)
net.LAM_3_34: To crush underfoot all the earth’s prisoners,
net.LAM_3_35: to deprive a person of his rights in the presence of the Most High,
net.LAM_3_36: to defraud a person in a lawsuit— the Lord does not approve of such things! מ (Mem)
net.LAM_3_37: Whose command was ever fulfilled unless the Lord decreed it?
net.LAM_3_38: Is it not from the mouth of the Most High that everything comes— both calamity and blessing?
net.LAM_3_39: Why should any living person complain when punished for his sins? נ (Nun)
net.LAM_3_40: Let us carefully examine our ways, and let us return to the Lord.
net.LAM_3_41: Let us lift up our hearts and our hands to God in heaven:
net.LAM_3_42: “We have blatantly rebelled; you have not forgiven.” ס (Samek)
net.LAM_3_43: You shrouded yourself with anger and then pursued us; you killed without mercy.
net.LAM_3_44: You shrouded yourself with a cloud so that no prayer could get through.
net.LAM_3_45: You make us like filthy scum in the estimation of the nations. פ (Pe)
net.LAM_3_46: All our enemies have gloated over us;
net.LAM_3_47: panic and pitfall have come upon us, devastation and destruction.
net.LAM_3_48: Streams of tears flow from my eyes because my people are destroyed. ע (Ayin)
net.LAM_3_49: Tears flow from my eyes and will not stop; there will be no break
net.LAM_3_50: until the Lord looks down from heaven and sees what has happened.
net.LAM_3_51: What my eyes see grieves me — all the suffering of the daughters in my city. צ (Tsade)
net.LAM_3_52: For no good reason my enemies hunted me down like a bird.
net.LAM_3_53: They shut me up in a pit and threw stones at me.
net.LAM_3_54: The waters closed over my head; I thought I was about to die. ק (Qof)
net.LAM_3_55: I have called on your name, O Lord, from the deepest pit.
net.LAM_3_56: You heard my plea: “Do not close your ears to my cry for relief!”
net.LAM_3_57: You came near on the day I called to you; you said, “Do not fear!” ר (Resh)
net.LAM_3_58: O Lord, you championed my cause; you redeemed my life.
net.LAM_3_59: You have seen the wrong done to me, O Lord; pronounce judgment on my behalf!
net.LAM_3_60: You have seen all their vengeance, all their plots against me. ש (Sin/Shin)
net.LAM_3_61: You have heard their taunts, O Lord, all their plots against me.
net.LAM_3_62: My assailants revile and conspire against me all day long.
net.LAM_3_63: Watch them from morning to evening; I am the object of their mocking songs. ת (Tav)
net.LAM_3_64: Pay them back what they deserve, O Lord, according to what they have done.
net.LAM_3_65: Give them a distraught heart; may your curse be on them!
net.LAM_3_66: Pursue them in anger and eradicate them from under the Lord’s heaven.
net.LAM_4_1: Alas! Gold has lost its luster; pure gold loses value. Jewels are scattered on every street corner. ב (Bet)
net.LAM_4_2: The precious sons of Zion were worth their weight in gold— Alas!—but now they are treated like broken clay pots, made by a potter. ג (Gimel)
net.LAM_4_3: Even the jackals nurse their young at their breast, but my people are cruel, like ostriches in the wilderness. ד (Dalet)
net.LAM_4_4: The infant’s tongue sticks to the roof of its mouth due to thirst; little children beg for bread, but no one gives them even a morsel. ה (He)
net.LAM_4_5: Those who once feasted on delicacies are now starving to death in the streets. Those who grew up wearing expensive clothes are now dying amid garbage. ו (Vav)
net.LAM_4_6: The punishment of my people exceeds that of Sodom, which was overthrown in a moment with no one to help her. ז (Zayin)
net.LAM_4_7: Our consecrated ones were brighter than snow, whiter than milk; their bodies more ruddy than corals, their hair like lapis lazuli. ח (Khet)
net.LAM_4_8: Now their appearance is darker than soot; they are not recognized in the streets. Their skin has shriveled on their bones; it is dried up, like tree bark. ט (Tet)
net.LAM_4_9: Those who die by the sword are better off than those who die of hunger, those who waste away, struck down from lack of food. י (Yod)
net.LAM_4_10: The hands of tenderhearted women cooked their own children, who became their food, when my people were destroyed. כ (Kaf)
net.LAM_4_11: The Lord fully vented his wrath; he poured out his fierce anger. He started a fire in Zion; it consumed her foundations. ל (Lamed)
net.LAM_4_12: Neither the kings of the earth nor the people of the lands ever thought that enemy or foe could enter the gates of Jerusalem. מ (Mem)
net.LAM_4_13: But it happened due to the sins of her prophets and the iniquities of her priests, who poured out in her midst the blood of the righteous. נ (Nun)
net.LAM_4_14: They wander blindly through the streets, defiled by the blood they shed, while no one dares to touch their garments. ס (Samek)
net.LAM_4_15: People cry to them, “Turn away! You are unclean! Turn away! Turn away! Don’t touch us!” So they have fled and wander about; but the nations say, “They may not stay here any longer.” פ (Pe)
net.LAM_4_16: The Lord himself has scattered them; he no longer watches over them. They did not honor the priests; they did not show favor to the elders. ע (Ayin) The People of Jerusalem Lament
net.LAM_4_17: Our eyes continually failed us as we looked in vain for help. From our watchtowers we watched for a nation that could not rescue us. צ (Tsade)
net.LAM_4_18: Our enemies hunted us down at every step so that we could not walk about in our streets. Our end drew near, our days were numbered, for our end had come! ק (Qof)
net.LAM_4_19: Those who pursued us were swifter than eagles in the sky. They chased us over the mountains; they ambushed us in the wilderness. ר (Resh)
net.LAM_4_20: Our very life breath—the Lord’s anointed king — was caught in their traps, of whom we thought, “Under his protection we will survive among the nations.” ש (Sin/Shin) The Prophet Speaks
net.LAM_4_21: Rejoice and be glad for now, O people of Edom, who reside in the land of Uz. But the cup of judgment will pass to you also; you will get drunk and take off your clothes. ת (Tav)
net.LAM_4_22: O people of Zion, your punishment will come to an end; he will not prolong your exile. But, O people of Edom, he will punish your sin and reveal your offenses!
net.LAM_5_1: O Lord, reflect on what has happened to us; consider and look at our disgrace.
net.LAM_5_2: Our inheritance is turned over to strangers; foreigners now occupy our homes.
net.LAM_5_3: We have become fatherless orphans; our mothers have become widows.
net.LAM_5_4: We must pay money for our own water; we must buy our own wood at a steep price.
net.LAM_5_5: We are pursued—they are breathing down our necks; we are weary and have no rest.
net.LAM_5_6: We have submitted to Egypt and Assyria in order to buy food to eat.
net.LAM_5_7: Our forefathers sinned and are dead, but we suffer their punishment.
net.LAM_5_8: Slaves rule over us; there is no one to rescue us from their power.
net.LAM_5_9: At the risk of our lives we get our food because robbers lurk in the wilderness.
net.LAM_5_10: Our skin is as hot as an oven due to a fever from hunger.
net.LAM_5_11: They raped women in Zion, virgins in the towns of Judah.
net.LAM_5_12: Princes were hung by their hands; elders were mistreated.
net.LAM_5_13: The young men perform menial labor; boys stagger from their labor.
net.LAM_5_14: The elders are gone from the city gate; the young men have stopped playing their music.
net.LAM_5_15: Our hearts no longer have any joy; our dancing is turned to mourning.
net.LAM_5_16: The crown has fallen from our head; woe to us, for we have sinned!
net.LAM_5_17: Because of this, our hearts are sick; because of these things, we can hardly see through our tears.
net.LAM_5_18: For wild animals are prowling over Mount Zion, which lies desolate.
net.LAM_5_19: But you, O Lord, reign forever; your throne endures from generation to generation.
net.LAM_5_20: Why do you keep on forgetting us? Why do you forsake us so long?
net.LAM_5_21: Bring us back to yourself, O Lord, so that we may return to you; renew our life as in days before,
net.LAM_5_22: unless you have utterly rejected us and are angry with us beyond measure.
